Community tool

Video Editor Community Post Generator

Draft feedback-first community posts that ask a real question, respect group rules, and send people to the most relevant Video Editor Jobs page with campaign tracking.

This tool supports the Reddit, Facebook, and forum launch loop by turning community replies into measurable editor profiles, hiring briefs, and content ideas.

Draft

Start with a useful community question

Reddit Feedback-first post

Post draft

Choose an angle and this tool will create a community-safe starting point with a tracked link.

Tracked link

The tracked URL will appear here.

Open target page Community page

Review the rules before posting. Use this as a starting point, then adapt it to the community tone.

Why feedback posts work better

Early community growth should ask for specific input, not announce a generic job board. Better comments reveal what editors and hiring teams actually need.

What to record after posting

Add the post URL, replies, submissions, quality notes, and next action to the Community Posts sheet so the next post gets sharper.

Answers

Questions editors and hiring teams ask first

What should I post in video editing communities?

Ask a specific feedback question about portfolios, job post quality, rates, workflow, or hiring briefs. Avoid dropping a generic launch link without context.

Can I use this for Reddit and Facebook groups?

Yes, but only when the post fits the community rules. Use the generated draft as a starting point and edit it to match the group norms.

Why use tracked links?

Tracked links help compare which communities, questions, and landing pages produce useful editor profiles or hiring briefs in the Source Summary sheet.
